behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a query has the dimensionality required by the vector index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is the handoff from real embedding output to index schema, not just local parsing logic. REAL-INFRA exists (external model API), but LLM-DEP is decisive because execution correctness depends on a real AI/ML model call. Slot A: "embedding vector ... has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index." Slot B: model-output boundary (real embedding API output). Slot C: if lowered to System with a deterministic fake embedding response, you miss provider/model dimension drift that only appears with real model output. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes one model-output contract check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with representative queries, then assert returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension configured in the vector DB; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: Similarity search returns results sorted by descending cosine similarity.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs often appear at the API/driver boundary between service code and the real vector DB response format. REAL-INFRA is decisive: real DB behavior (ANN engine defaults, score ordering semantics) can differ from in-process fakes. Slot A: "returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score." Slot B: real-infra boundary (real vector DB query/response behavior). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with a fake repo, tests can pass while production DB returns unsorted/ascending scores due to index/query defaults. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because this behavior is about deterministic score ordering, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real vector DB instance/index seeded with known vectors; issue a query vector and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing in the API response.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking changes at least some ordering for nuanced-intent queries versus raw similarity ranking.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a stubbed reranker can always force a reorder and hide whether the real model actually captures nuanced intent. LLM-DEP is decisive: correctness depends on real LLM output quality/variability. Slot A: "LLM re-ranking step changes the order ... when the query has nuanced intent." Slot B: model-output boundary (real LLM decision quality). Slot C: if lowered to System with fixed mock outputs, you cannot detect failures where the model stops reordering nuanced cases. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior specifies one agentic decision point (rerank), not a multi-agentic sequence under test.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated nuanced-intent query set and fixed candidate lists, run real LLM reranking, and assert that for at least a defined fraction of cases the final order differs from raw similarity order.

behaviors.description: No-close-match queries return an empty set instead of low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: deterministic fixtures for scores or embeddings can hide semantic false positives that appear only with real embedding/model behavior. REAL-INFRA exists, but LLM-DEP/model-output dependence is decisive because "no close matches" is a semantic quality condition driven by real model-produced vectors/scores. Slot A: "query with no close matches ... returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise." Slot B: model-output boundary (real embedding similarity behavior). Slot C: if lowered to System with synthetic vectors, threshold logic may pass while real embeddings still surface noisy neighbors. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior can be satisfied before reranking and does not require validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real embedding+vector search path on an out-of-domain/no-match query suite and assert result count is zero (or below strict threshold) under production relevance cutoff settings.

behaviors.description: Median end-to-end latency from query receipt to re-ranked response is under 2 seconds.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline SLO over sequential AI-involved stages (embedding call -> vector search -> LLM rerank) plus service orchestration, so MULTI-STEP is decisive. Slot A: "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds." Slot B: multi-step agentic boundary (multiple AI/ML steps in sequence). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic and measured only one model step in isolation, you miss compounded latency and queueing effects across the full chain that violate the median SLO.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like load against the real API path using real embedding and rerank providers plus real vector DB, collect per-request latency over a representative sample window, and assert p50 < 2.0s.
